title: Containers (digest-pinned) + lockfiles + provenance lint
context: Reproducibility requires digest-pinned containers, conda/renv lockfiles and a provenance lint that fails CI on incomplete manifests (PLAN §6/§8). This harness is a prerequisite for any end-to-end reproduction. Binding guardrails: open-access / aggregate / de-identified data only; controlled-access (dbGaP/EGA/biobanks) and identifiable patient data are out of scope; COSMIC/OncoKB/MSigDB-restricted are recipe-reference only; no clinical/diagnostic/prognostic/treatment language; research-only, not medical advice; provenance on every assertion.
objective: Add the reproducibility harness: digest-pinned container definitions, conda/renv lockfiles, seed control, and a provenance-manifest lint wired into CI.
acceptanceCriteria: All containers are pinned by digest; no ':latest' tags anywhere in the repo or CI.
Conda/renv (or equivalent) lockfiles are committed and used by the pipeline stub.
A provenance lint validates provenance.json against the ratified spec and fails CI on missing/invalid fields.
No secrets, tokens, or API keys appear in the repo, logs, or CI output; commit is DCO signed-off.
output: A pull request adding digest-pinned containers, lockfiles, seed control, and a CI provenance-lint gate.
